refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from the worksite mission context.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined multiple obligations: deriving, recording, and managing performance requirements. To ensure atomicity and solution neutrality, these functions are separated. This refined FR focuses solely on the derivation of performance requirements, which is a distinct functional need independent of how the requirements are recorded or managed.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can analyze worksite mission context inputs and produce a documented set of performance requirements such as cycle time, energy efficiency, and availability without specifying how these are recorded or managed.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall record performance requirements derived from the worksite mission context.
The NGHE solution shall manage performance requirements derived from the worksite mission context.

candidate_dps: Define a logical model to translate worksite mission context into performance requirements
Establish a traceable mapping framework linking worksite inputs to performance targets
Implement a version-controlled repository for performance baselines and calibration updates
Develop a validation process to ensure performance requirements comply with safety plans and operational policies
selected_dp.selected_dp: Define a logical model to translate worksite mission context into performance requirements

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on a logical model that isolates the translation of mission context to performance requirements, supporting independence from implementation details and other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability and accuracy of worksite mission context data
Risk that the logical model may become overly complex if too many variables are considered
Assumes that safety plans and operational policies are well-defined and accessible for integration
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including a DP for maintaining traceability and history of performance targets to fully meet acceptance criteria and support auditability.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the overall system logic and data processing components responsible for translating worksite mission context into performance requirements.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time data about the worksite and vehicle status to inform the logical model.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives mission tasking information that defines the worksite mission context.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts if performance requirements indicate safety concerns.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Facilitates communication with external systems and modules for data exchange.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as the logical model is central to the overall solution; minimal diffusion since it primarily uses internal data and interfaces.
Partial containment possible but the Vehicle module focuses more on physical control and actuation, leading to diffusion of logical modeling outside.
Teleoperation is focused on remote control and monitoring, not on mission context translation, causing high diffusion.
Safety Services may use performance requirements but does not define the logical model, leading to moderate diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for containing the DP because it is designed to handle system-level logic and data processing, including translating mission context into performance requirements, with minimal diffusion outside this module.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Safety Services if safety-related performance requirements are tightly coupled.
Risk of diffusion if Teleoperation or Vehicle modules require direct access to the logical model for real-time adjustments.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If diffusion risk is high, consider refining the DP to separate safety-critical performance requirements into a sub-DP that can be contained within Safety Services, improving modular clarity without becoming implementation-specific.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by defining a model that translates the worksite mission context into performance requirements, directly fulfilling the functional need to derive performance requirements from mission context.

diffusion_risks: Potential diffusion risk exists if Teleoperation or Vehicle modules require direct access to the logical model for real-time adjustments, as noted in the containment risks.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high containment (score 8) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating strong cohesion and appropriate packaging of the DP. Other modules show lower containment and higher diffusion, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the NGHE Solution module interfaces with Telemetry and Tasking inputs and produces SafetyAlert outputs, enabling traceability of performance targets to worksite inputs and history maintenance as per acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear with the NGHE Solution module owning the logical model and related data processing, minimizing overlap and diffusion with other modules.

recommended_revisions: Consider refining the DP to separate safety-critical performance requirements into a sub-DP contained within the Safety Services module to reduce potential overlap and diffusion.
Monitor and manage interface dependencies with Teleoperation and Vehicle modules to prevent diffusion of the logical model outside the NGHE Solution module.
Document and enforce clear interface contracts to maintain modular clarity and support verification of performance requirement derivation.

decision.justification: Although the current FR to DP to IM mapping passes all gates with strong justification and clear allocation, the high safety criticality and identified diffusion risks suggest that further decomposition is warranted. Specifically, refining the DP to separate safety-critical performance requirements into a sub-DP within the Safety Services module will reduce overlap and diffusion, improving modular clarity and safety assurance. This next-level decomposition will enable more precise verification and risk management aligned with safety needs.
next_level_sub_frs: Derive performance requirements related to telemetry and tasking inputs
Derive performance requirements related to safety alert outputs

revise.why: To separate safety-critical performance requirements into a dedicated sub-DP contained within the Safety Services module, reducing diffusion and overlap and enhancing safety assurance.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate IM boundary for the logical model.
Telemetry, Tasking, and SafetyAlert interfaces are well-defined and traceable.
Safety criticality requires stricter modular separation to manage risks.
open_risks: Potential diffusion risk if Teleoperation or Vehicle modules access the logical model directly.
Interface dependencies may cause diffusion outside NGHE Solution module.
evidence_needed: Verification results demonstrating traceability of performance targets to worksite inputs.
Documentation of interface contracts enforcing modular boundaries.
Analysis of diffusion risk mitigation after sub-DP separation.
